items.summary: Gulf Arab oil producers are cutting production as they run out of storage space because they can't export through the Strait of Hormuz

items.headline: Anthropic sues Trump administration over Pentagon blacklist
items.summary: The lawsuit says Anthropic is being harmed "irreparably" and could lose hundreds of millions of dollars.

items.headline: K-shaped economy puts pressure on women, as lower pay and higher costs reduce spending
items.summary: Women are under pressure from lower pay and reduced affordability on everyday items, which is widening the K-shaped economy and seeing women spending less.

items.headline: Treasury yields are little changed as traders weigh inflation and recession risks
items.summary: U.S. Treasury yields moved higher and later pulled back on Monday as oil prices moved past $100 a barrel and later pared those gains.

items.headline: My boyfriend and I broke up, and he won’t communicate with me about child support. What can I do?
items.summary: “I earn $100, 000 and I’m currently shouldering the mortgage, maintenance — specific to a co-op — and normal kid expenses.”

items.headline: Oil tankers transiting Strait of Hormuz 'must be very careful,' Iran Foreign Ministry warns
items.summary: The price of crude oil has sharply spiked as the Strait of Hormuz has been effectively closed as the United States and Israel wage war on Iran.

items.headline: Ticketmaster parent Live Nation reaches settlement with Department of Justice over antitrust concerns
items.summary: Live Nation, which owns Ticketmaster, reached a settlement with the Department of Justice over antitrust concerns.

items.headline: Here’s how Amazon just stepped up its robotaxi competition with Tesla and Google
items.summary: Amazon’s Zoox is testing robotaxi operations in 10 U.S. cities, but Google’s Waymo has a big lead.

items.headline: Amazon's Zoox expands robotaxi testing to Phoenix and Dallas
items.summary: Zoox will start by deploying a fleet of retrofitted Toyota Highlander SUVs, before rolling out its toaster-shaped robotaxis for testing.

items.headline: Higher oil prices will push U.S. inflation rate to 3% this year, El-Erian says
items.summary: Higher prices will push inflation upward in 2025, limiting the Federal Reserve’s ability to cushion the soft U.S. labor market, said former Pimco CEO Mohamed El-Erian.

items.headline: Oil prices pull back from highest levels since 2022 on talk of G-7 emergency release of crude reserves
items.summary: Crude prices were surging on Monday amid signs that the Iran conflict wasn’t de-escalating, and as some major oil producers were reportedly forced to cut production.

items.headline: Hims & Hers stock skyrockets 40% as Novo Nordisk ends feud, agrees to sell Ozempic and Wegovy on platform
items.summary: Hims & Hers shares jumped more than 50% in premarket trade on Monday after ending a dispute with Novo Nordisk by striking a partnership with the Danish drug giant.

items.headline: Microsoft has a new Anthropic partnership. Will Claude Cowork offer an AI advantage?
items.summary: Microsoft is evolving its Copilot offering from chatbot to agent through a new arrangement with Anthropic.

items.headline: Airline stocks dive as oil-price spike could trigger a tipping point for travelers
items.summary: Airline stocks were set to continue their recent sharp selloff amid fears that travel demand will drop as the escalating Middle East conflict sends crude oil prices above $100 a barrel.

items.summary: Rising oil prices can affect the stock market in significant ways.

items.headline: Seventh U.S. service member killed in Iran war ID'd as Sgt. Benjamin Pennington
items.summary: Pennington, 26, from Glendale, Kentucky, was wounded on March 1 during an Iranian strike at Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia. He died on Sunday.

items.headline: New Ticketmaster settlement may be good news for Wall Street, but bad news for concertgoers
items.summary: Shares of Ticketmaster parent Live Nation rallied toward their biggest gain in nearly a year, as analysts don’t see the settlement as helping competition to gain traction.

items.headline: European markets close lower as oil price surges amid Middle East turmoil

items.headline: Anthropic ratchets up its Pentagon battle as it sues the government

items.headline: The Iran war has put the brakes on the next Bank of England rate cut

items.headline: Oil shock prompts South Korea to impose fuel price cap for the first time in 30 years

items.headline: Small 401(k)s may automatically follow workers to their next job — except Roth money
items.summary: A network of plan administrators helps small 401(k) accounts rolled into IRAs find their way to a worker's next workplace plan — unless it's Roth money.
